t1: Where does the protein end up inside the cell? DeepLoc 2.1 (DTU Health Tech) uses a protein language model (ProtT5) to predict subcellular localization and membrane association from sequence alone. For a GPI-anchored extracellular protein like stereocilin, we expect: extracellular localization + lipid anchor (GPI) or soluble.
t2: Result: Both IgK-SP + Mini-STRC (700-1775) and IgK-SP + Ultra-Mini (1075-1775) are correctly predicted as extracellular with lipid anchor (GPI) association. The lipid anchor probability rises from 28.4 % (full STRC, disordered N-terminus crowds the GPI signal) to 72.1 % (Mini-STRC) and stays ≥ 70 % for Ultra-Mini — the shorter the construct, the more prominent the GPI signal to the model. The no-SP control loses extracellular localisation (drops to Cell membrane), confirming the signal peptide is essential for proper trafficking in both constructs.
t3: IgK-SP + Mini-STRC 700-1775 (first-pass · same prediction for Ultra-Mini)
t5: DeepLoc 2.1 attention maps showing which residues contribute to localization prediction. Note the strong N-terminal signal peptide attention and C-terminal GPI signal.
t23: 15. Subcellular localisation prediction (applies to both constructs) DeepLoc 2.1
